Document identified risks in a risk register with risk ratings and affected entity/supplier categories.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Procurement team: provide complete supplier register with tier-1 and tier-2+ vendor lists, countries of operation, and product categories; Operations team: submit organisational structure and operational facility locations; Risk/Compliance team: deliver modern slavery risk assessment report with methodology, risk ratings, and supply chain vulnerability analysis.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Suppliers (tier-1 and accessible tier-2): complete modern slavery risk self-assessment questionnaires; industry associations or external risk databases: provide sector-specific modern slavery prevalence reports for geographic and industry benchmarking.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Supplier risk register with risk scores, modern slavery risk assessment methodology documentation, supply chain mapping diagrams showing geographic and sector distribution, sector-specific risk benchmarking reports, evidence of risk categorisation process (spreadsheets, workshop notes).

key_requirements.text: Describe actions taken to assess and address identified modern slavery risks
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Public naming for non-compliant entities; expected enhanced penalties under planned amendments.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Remediation/Compliance team: develop and document specific actions (e.g. supplier audits, contractual modern slavery clauses, training, grievance mechanisms) aligned to each identified risk category. 2. Document the causal link between each risk and the mitigation action taken, with implementation timeline and responsible owner. 3. Ensure actions address both prevention and remediation pathways.
key_requirements.internal_actions: HR/Training team: provide modern slavery awareness and reporting training records; Procurement team: deliver evidence of modern slavery contractual clauses inserted into supplier terms; Compliance team: compile action register linking risks to mitigation actions with owners and timelines; Internal Audit: provide audit reports on supplier compliance.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Suppliers: submit audit/assessment results, confirmation of modern slavery clause acceptance in contracts, training completion certificates, and evidence of grievance mechanism availability.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Modern slavery action plan (linked to identified risks), supplier audit reports, signed supplier agreements with modern slavery clauses, employee training records and completion certificates, grievance mechanism documentation, internal action tracker with implementation dates and owner accountability.

key_requirements.text: Assess effectiveness of actions through measurable key performance indicators
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Public naming for non-compliant entities; expected enhanced penalties under planned amendments.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Compliance/Risk team: define 2–4 measurable KPIs for each mitigation action (e.g. % of suppliers audited, grievance reports received and resolved, training completion rate, contract compliance rate). 2. Collect quarterly or annual data against each KPI and compare to baseline or targets. 3. Document performance results and variance explanations in an effectiveness report attached to or referenced in the modern slavery statement.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Compliance team: establish KPI dashboard or tracking spreadsheet with baseline and targets; HR team: provide training completion and grievance reports; Procurement team: deliver supplier audit completion rates and compliance metrics; Finance team: allocate budget tracking for modern slavery remediation spend.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Suppliers: provide audit completion confirmations, grievance data (if applicable), and evidence of contractual obligation fulfilment against agreed KPIs.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: KPI definition and baseline document, quarterly or annual KPI performance reports with actual results vs. targets, variance analysis explanations, grievance and remediation outcome logs, supplier audit completion trackers, effectiveness summary section of modern slavery statement.

key_requirements.text: Consult with entities owned or controlled by the reporting entity
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Public naming for non-compliant entities; expected enhanced penalties under planned amendments.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Corporate Governance/Company Secretary: identify all entities owned or controlled by the reporting entity (subsidiaries, joint ventures, franchisees per legal definition). 2. Conduct consultation meetings with each identified entity to gather modern slavery risk data, actions, and effectiveness evidence. 3. Consolidate consultation findings into the statement and retain records of consultation requests, meeting notes, and responses.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Company Secretary: provide corporate structure chart and list of all controlled entities with ownership thresholds; General Counsel: confirm legal definition of 'control' and identify all in-scope entities; Finance team: provide shareholding and governance records; each subsidiary/controlled entity: submit local modern slavery risk assessment and mitigation actions.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Controlled entities (subsidiaries, JVs): provide completed modern slavery questionnaires, risk assessments, and action summaries; external corporate structure advisors (if engaged): confirm entity control classification.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Corporate structure diagram showing ownership and control relationships, list of consulted entities with control basis documented, consultation request emails and follow-up records, subsidiary/entity responses to consultation (questionnaires or reports), consolidated consultation summary section in modern slavery statement, board minutes confirming consultation completion.

key_requirements.text: Conduct supplier due diligence and require suppliers to report on their own modern slavery risks
key_requirements.consequence: Unlawful data transfers or inadequate vendor contracts expose the organisation to fines. Penalties: Public naming for non-compliant entities; expected enhanced penalties under planned amendments.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Vendor contracts/DPAs; third-party risk assessments; supplier due diligence records; data transfer agreements
how_to_meet: 1. Develop and deploy a standardised Modern Slavery Risk Assessment questionnaire covering labour practices, subcontracting, and geographic risk factors; require all new and existing suppliers above a defined spend threshold to complete and return within 30 days.
2. Establish a documented supplier onboarding process that includes review of assessment responses against internal risk criteria, and classify suppliers into risk tiers (low, medium, high).
3. For medium and high-risk suppliers, contractually mandate annual modern slavery certification or attestation, and conduct desktop or on-site audits based on risk rating.
internal_actions: Procurement team: design and maintain the Modern Slavery Risk Assessment template and scoring rubric; create a supplier risk register with assessment dates and risk classifications.
Legal team: embed modern slavery reporting and audit rights clauses into standard supplier contract terms and conditions.
Finance team: maintain a master supplier database linked to risk tier and assessment status for audit trail purposes.
vendor_actions: All suppliers: complete and sign the Modern Slavery Risk Assessment questionnaire, providing evidence of labour policies, workforce composition, and subcontractor vetting practices.
High-risk suppliers: provide annual written certification of modern slavery compliance or engage a third-party auditor to conduct a modern slavery audit and share the report.
compliance_evidence: Completed and signed Modern Slavery Risk Assessment forms from all active suppliers with dated submission records.
Supplier risk register showing assessment completion dates, risk tier assignments, and review evidence.
Executed supplier contracts containing modern slavery due diligence and reporting obligations.
Copies of supplier annual attestations, certifications, or third-party audit reports for high-risk vendors.
Internal audit trails documenting supplier onboarding review decisions and risk classification rationale.

key_requirements.text: Provide training to procurement and supply chain staff on modern slavery indicators
key_requirements.consequence: Unlawful data transfers or inadequate vendor contracts expose the organisation to fines. Penalties: Public naming for non-compliant entities; expected enhanced penalties under planned amendments.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Vendor contracts/DPAs; third-party risk assessments; supplier due diligence records; data transfer agreements
how_to_meet: 1. Develop a targeted training module covering modern slavery risk indicators (wage theft, forced labour, child labour, restrictions on movement, excessive working hours), practical supply chain scenarios, and reporting mechanisms; align content to the organisation's supply chain and geographic footprint.
2. Deliver mandatory training to all procurement, supply chain, sourcing, and contract management staff; conduct annual refresher sessions and induct new team members within 90 days of hire.
3. Maintain training records with attendance logs, completion dates, and assessment scores; track and report training metrics to senior management quarterly.
internal_actions: HR/Learning & Development team: design and host the modern slavery training module (in-person, online, or blended); maintain an enrolment and attendance register with completion dates and participant names.
Procurement team: identify all staff roles requiring training and provide the current roster to HR; designate a training champion to track uptake and flag non-compliance.
Compliance/Legal team: review training content for accuracy against the Modern Slavery Act and provide sign-off before delivery.
vendor_actions: Applicable third-party logistics, recruitment, or labour supply vendors: confirm in writing that their staff handling the organisation's supply chain have received equivalent modern slavery training or agree to attend the organisation's training session.
compliance_evidence: Training module curriculum or presentation materials with learning objectives aligned to modern slavery risk indicators.
Training attendance register(s) listing participants, dates, duration, and completion status for each session.
Training completion certificates or quiz/assessment scores demonstrating knowledge retention.
Annual training metrics report showing completion rates, trends, and any non-compliance flags.
Signed confirmation from relevant third-party service providers that their staff have received modern slavery training.

regulations.penalties: Public naming for non-compliant entities; expected enhanced penalties under planned amendments

regulations.name: Canada Fighting Against Forced Labour and Child Labour in Supply Chains Act (S-211)
regulations.authority: Public Safety Canada
regulations.summary: Canada's Supply Chains Act (Bill S-211), effective January 2024, requires federal government institutions and large private sector entities to report annually on measures taken to prevent and reduce the risk of forced labour and child labour in their supply chains. Reports must be submitted to the Minister of Public Safety and are publicly available.

key_requirements.text: Submit annual report to Public Safety Canada by May 31 each year covering supply chain forced labour risks
key_requirements.consequence: Unlawful data transfers or inadequate vendor contracts expose the organisation to fines. Penalties: Fines up to CAD 250,000 for individuals and entities for false or misleading reports; management liability for knowing violations.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Vendor contracts/DPAs; third-party risk assessments; supplier due diligence records; data transfer agreements
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Legal team: establish annual reporting calendar with May 31 deadline and assign accountability owner. 2. Compliance team: compile supply chain risk assessment data, policy documentation, and remediation records into report template. 3. Finance team: submit final report to Public Safety Canada via designated portal before deadline.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Legal team: provide signed calendar reminder and filing checklist. Compliance team: deliver consolidated risk assessment spreadsheet, policy inventory, and supporting evidence file. Finance team: confirm submission receipt and tracking number from Public Safety Canada.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Supply chain partners: provide annual attestations confirming no forced or child labour incidents and certify compliance with company labour standards policy.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Submission confirmation letter from Public Safety Canada, timestamped filing receipt, complete annual report PDF with all required sections, internal approval memo from designated reporting authority.

key_requirements.text: Report must be approved by the governing board and publicly available on company website
key_requirements.consequence: Lack of designated responsibility can result in personal liability for senior management. Penalties: Fines up to CAD 250,000 for individuals and entities for false or misleading reports; management liability for knowing violations.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Board/committee minutes; appointment letters; terms of reference; senior management sign-off records; governance frameworks
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Board Secretary: schedule annual forced labour report for board meeting agenda and ensure distribution to all directors at least 5 business days before vote. 2. Legal team: prepare board resolution template documenting approval and required disclosures. 3. Communications team: publish approved report on company website homepage or dedicated sustainability page with publication date.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Board Secretary: provide signed board minutes and resolution documenting approval vote. Legal team: deliver executed board resolution with officer signature. Communications/Marketing team: confirm website publication with URL, accessibility audit, and content management system records.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Website hosting provider: confirm content is publicly accessible and provide technical hosting certificate or SLA confirmation.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Signed and certified board minutes, board resolution with approval vote count and date, website publication screenshot with timestamp, Google/Bing search index confirmation showing public accessibility.

key_requirements.text: Identify structure, activities, and supply chains of the entity
key_requirements.consequence: Unlawful data transfers or inadequate vendor contracts expose the organisation to fines. Penalties: Fines up to CAD 250,000 for individuals and entities for false or misleading reports; management liability for knowing violations.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Vendor contracts/DPAs; third-party risk assessments; supplier due diligence records; data transfer agreements
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Finance/Operations team: document legal entity structure including all subsidiaries, joint ventures, and operating divisions with ownership percentages. 2. Supply Chain team: create detailed supply chain map identifying primary, secondary, and tertiary suppliers by geography, product category, and materials sourced. 3. Compliance team: integrate structure and supply chain data into annual report narrative with visual diagrams or tables.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Finance team: provide consolidated org chart, subsidiaries register, and entity master file. Operations team: deliver supply chain network diagram with supplier tiers, sourcing locations, and product categories. Supply Chain team: compile supplier directory with contact information and spend data.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: All tier-1 suppliers: confirm their tier-2 and tier-3 supplier lists and key sourcing countries in writing.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Org chart showing all entities as of report date, supply chain map with legend and source documentation, supplier spreadsheet with tier classification, signed supplier questionnaires confirming sub-supplier disclosure.

key_requirements.text: Describe policies and due diligence processes relating to forced and child labour
key_requirements.consequence: Unlawful data transfers or inadequate vendor contracts expose the organisation to fines. Penalties: Fines up to CAD 250,000 for individuals and entities for false or misleading reports; management liability for knowing violations.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Vendor contracts/DPAs; third-party risk assessments; supplier due diligence records; data transfer agreements
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. HR/Legal team: document all policies addressing forced labour, child labour, wages, working hours, and safe conditions; include policy approval dates and version control. 2. Compliance/Procurement team: detail due diligence procedures such as supplier screening, audit protocols, KPI monitoring, and incident escalation workflows. 3. Compliance team: synthesize policies and procedures into written narrative sections for annual report with cross-references to policy documents.
key_requirements.internal_actions: HR team: provide current Code of Conduct, Labour Standards Policy, and any collective bargaining agreements. Legal team: deliver Supplier Code of Conduct and contractual terms addressing forced/child labour. Compliance team: submit due diligence procedure documentation, audit templates, and risk assessment frameworks with approval dates.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: All suppliers: sign attestation confirming receipt and understanding of company Labour Standards Policy and commitment to compliance verification processes.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Current version copies of Code of Conduct, Labour Standards Policy, and Supplier Code of Conduct with approval signatures and dates, due diligence procedure manual, supplier attestation letters, policy distribution records.

key_requirements.text: Identify parts of business or supply chains that carry risk of forced or child labour
key_requirements.consequence: Unlawful data transfers or inadequate vendor contracts expose the organisation to fines. Penalties: Fines up to CAD 250,000 for individuals and entities for false or misleading reports; management liability for knowing violations.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Vendor contracts/DPAs; third-party risk assessments; supplier due diligence records; data transfer agreements
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Compliance/Supply Chain team: conduct risk assessment across all business units and supplier tiers, scoring exposure by geography, industry sector, labour intensity, and regulatory environment using a documented methodology. 2. Legal team: identify and classify high-risk supply chains (e.g., construction, agriculture, textiles, manufacturing in high-risk countries) with supporting evidence. 3. Compliance team: document risk findings and mitigation measures in annual report with clear identification of high-risk vs. lower-risk segments.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Supply Chain team: provide completed risk assessment matrix with scoring methodology, country-level risk ratings, and supplier risk classifications. Compliance team: deliver risk register identifying all high-risk suppliers, products, and countries by business unit. Legal team: confirm legal review of identified risks and applicable jurisdictional regulations.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: High-risk suppliers: submit detailed responses to risk assessment questionnaire covering workforce composition, labour practices, recruitment processes, and subcontractor transparency.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Risk assessment methodology document, completed risk assessment matrix with scoring, risk register spreadsheet, country risk profile summaries, supplier risk questionnaire responses, internal risk review sign-off.

key_requirements.text: Describe steps taken to remediate any forced or child labour that has occurred
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Fines up to CAD 250,000 for individuals and entities for false or misleading reports; management liability for knowing violations.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Compliance/HR team: establish incident investigation and remediation procedures documenting any reported or discovered cases of forced or child labour, including investigation findings, root causes, and remedial actions. 2. Legal team: ensure all remediation steps are documented (e.g., worker restitution, supplier termination, corrective action plans, third-party audits) with supporting records. 3. Compliance team: describe remediation narrative in annual report including any incidents identified, remedial measures taken, and preventive actions to avoid recurrence.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Compliance team: provide incident log, investigation files, and remediation action plans for any reported forced/child labour cases. HR team: supply documentation of worker support or compensation provided. Legal team: deliver correspondence confirming supplier notifications, corrective action requirements, or contract terminations. Audit team: supply third-party audit reports or investigations related to remediation verification.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Any suppliers subject to remediation: provide signed corrective action plans, evidence of remediation completion (e.g., process improvements, training records, worker interviews), and commit to third-party verification if required.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Incident investigation reports with findings, remediation action plans with completion dates, worker support/compensation records, supplier correspondence and corrective action commitments, third-party audit or verification reports confirming remediation, signed remediation closure confirmations.

key_requirements.text: Describe training provided to employees on forced labour
key_requirements.consequence: Insufficient staff training undermines compliance and may be cited in regulatory findings. Penalties: Fines up to CAD 250,000 for individuals and entities for false or misleading reports; management liability for knowing violations.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Training completion records; awareness programme materials; staff acknowledgement logs; competency assessments
how_to_meet: 1. Design mandatory training curriculum covering forced labour indicators, reporting mechanisms, and employee rights; deliver to all staff with documented completion records.
2. Tailor training content by role (e.g., procurement, HR, operations) to address supply chain and labour practices relevant to each function.
3. Conduct annual refresher training and maintain records of attendance, dates, and assessment scores for all participants.
internal_actions: HR team: provide employee roster and current training records; identify gaps in coverage.
Legal team: draft training content outline addressing forced labour definitions, warning signs, and escalation procedures.
Learning & Development team: deliver training via LMS or workshops and export completion reports with employee names, dates, and pass/fail status.
vendor_actions: Third-party training providers: deliver customized forced labour awareness sessions and provide attendance certificates and completion rosters.
Suppliers and contractors: provide evidence of forced labour training delivered to their own staff and confirm inclusion in their annual compliance cycle.
compliance_evidence: Training curriculum document with learning objectives and content outline.
LMS completion reports listing all employees, training dates, and pass/fail results.
Training attendance sign-in sheets or email confirmations.
Assessment scores or quiz results demonstrating knowledge retention.
Third-party trainer certificates of delivery and participant rosters.

key_requirements.text: Applies to entities listed on Canadian stock exchange, or with assets, revenue, or employees above set thresholds
key_requirements.consequence: Insufficient staff training undermines compliance and may be cited in regulatory findings. Penalties: Fines up to CAD 250,000 for individuals and entities for false or misleading reports; management liability for knowing violations.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Training completion records; awareness programme materials; staff acknowledgement logs; competency assessments
how_to_meet: 1. Determine whether the organization meets any of the three thresholds: listed on Canadian securities exchange, or total assets ≥CAD$20 million, revenue ≥CAD$40 million, or ≥250 employees.
2. Document the threshold assessment with audited financial statements or official exchange listing confirmation and retain for audit trail.
3. If above any threshold, include the organization on the compliance register and apply S-211 disclosure and reporting requirements; reassess annually.
internal_actions: Finance team: provide most recent audited financial statements showing total assets and revenue; confirm fiscal year-end and audit date.
Company Secretary or Governance team: confirm current listing status on Canadian securities exchange (TSX, TSX Venture) or provide proof of delisting.
HR team: provide current total headcount across all operating entities and jurisdictions.
Compliance team: create a threshold assessment matrix documenting which criteria are met and assign responsibility for annual reassessment.
vendor_actions: External auditor: confirm audit opinion and sign-off on financial statements used for threshold determination.
Canadian Securities Exchange (if applicable): confirm active listing status in writing or provide official registration records.
compliance_evidence: Most recent audited financial statements (balance sheet and income statement) showing total assets and revenue.
Audit opinion letter from independent external auditor.
Official Canadian securities exchange listing confirmation or delisting notice.
Internal threshold assessment memo dated and signed by CFO and General Counsel.
Annual reassessment checklist completed each fiscal year documenting continued compliance status.

regulations.penalties: Fines up to CAD 250,000 for individuals and entities for false or misleading reports; management liability for knowing violations

regulations.name: Spain Law 2/2023 on Whistleblower Protection
regulations.authority: Independent Whistleblower Protection Authority (Autoridad Independiente de Proteccion del Informante, A.A.I.)
regulations.summary: Spain's Law 2/2023 transposes the EU Whistleblowing Directive (2019/1937), requiring public entities and private companies with 50+ employees to establish and operate internal reporting channels for regulatory breaches. Introduces comprehensive protections for whistleblowers and significant penalties for retaliation.

key_requirements.text: Establish a confidential internal reporting channel accessible to employees and contractors
key_requirements.consequence: Unlawful data transfers or inadequate vendor contracts expose the organisation to fines. Penalties: Fines up to EUR 1 million for retaliation against whistleblowers; fines up to EUR 300,000 for failure to establish reporting channels.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Vendor contracts/DPAs; third-party risk assessments; supplier due diligence records; data transfer agreements
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Procure or develop a secure reporting platform (email alias, hotline, or dedicated portal) that encrypts submissions and restricts access to designated handlers. 2. Publish the channel details in employee handbooks, intranet, and contractor onboarding materials with clear instructions for use. 3. Test the channel quarterly to verify functionality, encryption, and access controls.
key_requirements.internal_actions: IT team: configure dedicated encrypted mailbox or portal with multi-factor authentication and audit logging; HR team: draft communication templates and distribution schedule for all staff; Legal team: confirm channel complies with GDPR data retention rules.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: External hotline provider (if applicable): provide written commitment to data encryption, uptime SLA (≥99%), and segregation of personally identifiable information; platform vendor: sign Data Processing Agreement confirming GDPR compliance and no third-party data sharing.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Signed platform Data Processing Agreement, channel access control matrix, quarterly functionality test reports, employee communication logs with timestamps, and evidence of publication in staff handbook and intranet.

key_requirements.text: Designate a responsible person or team to manage reports and maintain confidentiality
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Fines up to EUR 1 million for retaliation against whistleblowers; fines up to EUR 300,000 for failure to establish reporting channels.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Issue a formal role description appointing a named individual or small cross-functional team (Legal, Compliance, HR) as the Whistleblower Report Handler with defined responsibilities and escalation paths. 2. Establish written Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) documenting receipt, logging, investigation initiation, and confidentiality safeguards for all reports. 3. Provide mandatory training to handlers on confidentiality obligations, conflict-of-interest management, and handling sensitive information.
key_requirements.internal_actions: HR team: issue appointment letter and role description for handler(s); Legal team: draft and approve SOP document; Compliance team: develop and deliver training module with attendance records; IT team: configure segregated log and case management system with role-based access.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: External legal advisor (if involved in investigations): sign confidentiality agreement and confirm handling protocols align with Spanish whistleblower protection law; case management system vendor: provide audit trail documentation and confirm data segregation capabilities.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Signed appointment letters or role descriptions, approved SOP document with version control, training attendance records with dates, handler confidentiality agreements, and IT access control logs showing segregated permissions.

key_requirements.text: Acknowledge reports within 7 days and communicate follow-up actions within 3 months
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Fines up to EUR 1 million for retaliation against whistleblowers; fines up to EUR 300,000 for failure to establish reporting channels.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Configure automated acknowledgment messages that confirm receipt within one business day and provide a reference number; implement a case tracking log with required response deadlines (7 days for receipt confirmation, 3 months for follow-up). 2. Establish escalation procedures to ensure overdue responses trigger management review and notify the whistleblower of any delays with explanations. 3. Document all communications sent to the reporter, including dates and content, in a centralized register.
key_requirements.internal_actions: IT team: configure automated email acknowledgment system and case tracker with deadline alerts; Compliance team: define escalation triggers and approval workflows for delayed responses; Legal team: create communication templates for 7-day acknowledgment and 3-month follow-up letters.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Case management system vendor: confirm system generates automatic deadline reminders and audit trails showing all communications; external investigation firm (if used): commit contractually to adhering to 3-month reporting timeline and provide weekly status updates.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Case tracking spreadsheet or system export showing receipt dates, acknowledgment dates, follow-up dates, and any delays with justifications; sample acknowledgment emails and follow-up letters; system-generated deadline alert logs; communication register signed off by handler.

key_requirements.text: Implement strong confidentiality protections for the whistleblower's identity
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Fines up to EUR 1 million for retaliation against whistleblowers; fines up to EUR 300,000 for failure to establish reporting channels.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Implement technical controls: encrypt all stored report data using AES-256 or equivalent, restrict database access to handlers via role-based credentials, and use secure communication channels (TLS 1.2+ for email/portal transmissions). 2. Adopt administrative controls: establish access logs, limit personnel with viewing rights to essential handlers, use pseudonyms in internal documentation where possible, and segregate whistleblower identity data from investigation details. 3. Conduct annual penetration testing or security assessment focused on the reporting system.
key_requirements.internal_actions: IT team: implement encryption standards, configure access controls, and enable comprehensive audit logging for all report access; Data Protection Officer: conduct Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) for the reporting channel; Compliance team: document pseudonym protocols and handler confidentiality pledges.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Hosting/platform provider: provide ISO 27001 certificate and written security assurance covering encryption, access controls, and incident response procedures; external security auditor: deliver annual penetration test report covering the whistleblower system.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: DPIA document approved by DPO, encryption configuration documentation, access control matrix with handler names and permissions, audit logs showing access dates/times/users, ISO 27001 or equivalent security certification, annual penetration test summary, and handler signed confidentiality pledges.

key_requirements.text: Prohibit any form of retaliation against whistleblowers including dismissal, demotion, or harassment
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Fines up to EUR 1 million for retaliation against whistleblowers; fines up to EUR 300,000 for failure to establish reporting channels.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Draft and publish a formal Anti-Retaliation Policy prohibiting dismissal, demotion, pay cuts, reassignment, harassment, or any adverse employment action against whistleblowers; ensure it covers employees, contractors, and third-party workers and references Spanish legal protections. 2. Integrate anti-retaliation obligations into management training, performance review procedures, and disciplinary policies; document that managers sign acknowledgment of the policy. 3. Establish a mechanism for the whistleblower to report suspected retaliation to an independent body (e.g., separate from the original handler) with documented follow-up within 30 days.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Legal team: draft Anti-Retaliation Policy and ensure alignment with Spanish Labor Law and Law 2/2023; HR team: integrate policy into employee handbook, contract templates, and manager onboarding with signed acknowledgments; Compliance team: develop manager training curriculum and track attendance; Internal Audit: design retaliation monitoring procedures.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: External employment law counsel: review policy for Spanish law compliance and provide written confirmation; external investigator (if retaliation claims are handled externally): commit to confidentiality and independence from original investigation handler.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Published Anti-Retaliation Policy in employee handbook and intranet with version date, signed policy acknowledgments from all managers, training completion certificates, independent reporting mechanism documentation, and any retaliation complaint logs with investigation summaries and corrective actions taken.

key_requirements.text: Allow anonymous reporting channels where technically feasible
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Fines up to EUR 1 million for retaliation against whistleblowers; fines up to EUR 300,000 for failure to establish reporting channels.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Configure the primary reporting channel to accept anonymous submissions by removing or not requiring name/email fields, or provide a parallel anonymous email alias (e.g., anonymous@company.com) that does not log sender IP addresses. 2. Ensure the case management system can track and investigate reports without revealing the whistleblower's identity to non-essential personnel and use generic reference numbers in all communications. 3. Document technical feasibility assessment; if true anonymity is not possible due to organizational size or system constraints, document the limitation and offer an anonymous legal external reporting option (e.g., external ethics hotline provider with anonymization).
key_requirements.internal_actions: IT team: configure reporting portal to accept optional or no-identity fields, disable IP logging for anonymous submissions, and test anonymous submission workflows; Legal team: document technical feasibility assessment and compliance approach; Compliance team: establish procedures for handling anonymous reports without breaching confidentiality.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Anonymous reporting platform vendor (if used): provide technical documentation confirming IP masking, no identity logging, and encryption of submissions; external legal hotline provider (backup option): confirm ability to accept anonymous reports and provide anonymized forwarding to the organization.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Technical feasibility assessment document signed by IT and Legal, screenshots or configuration documentation showing optional identity fields, audit logs confirming IP logging is disabled for anonymous channel, sample anonymous reports with investigation outcomes, vendor technical documentation, and external hotline agreement referencing anonymity.

key_requirements.text: Publish information about reporting channels in a clear and accessible format
key_requirements.consequence: Missing or inadequate policies may be cited in regulatory audits and lead to sanctions. Penalties: Fines up to EUR 1 million for retaliation against whistleblowers; fines up to EUR 300,000 for failure to establish reporting channels.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Policy register; Records of Processing Activities; consent management records; privacy notices; data subject request logs
how_to_meet: 1. Designate a compliance owner to create and maintain a centralised whistleblowing disclosure document listing all available reporting channels (internal hotline, email, online portal, external authority contacts) with clear access instructions.

2. Publish this information on the company intranet, employee handbook, corporate website, and physical notice boards in at least Spanish and English, ensuring accessibility for employees with disabilities (WCAG 2.1 AA compliance).
3. Conduct a quarterly audit to confirm all channels remain functional, accessible, and properly staffed with documented response protocols.
internal_actions: Legal team: draft and sign off the whistleblowing disclosure statement identifying all reporting channels, languages supported, and contact details.
HR team: integrate whistleblowing channel information into onboarding materials and distribute to all current employees with signed acknowledgment records.
IT team: publish the disclosure on the intranet and corporate website with accessibility audit reports; maintain audit logs confirming publication date and any updates.
Facilities team: post printed notices in visible employee areas with photographic evidence of placement.
vendor_actions: External hotline provider: supply service level agreement confirming 24/7 availability, multilingual support, and secure recording protocols; provide quarterly uptime and call-handling statistics.
Website hosting vendor: certify WCAG 2.1 AA compliance for the published disclosure page via accessibility audit report.
Translation vendor (if applicable): provide certified translations of whistleblowing disclosure in all required languages with notarised statements of accuracy.
compliance_evidence: Signed whistleblowing disclosure statement with all reporting channels and access instructions.
Screenshots or PDFs of intranet, website, and employee handbook pages showing published information with timestamps.
Photographic evidence of physical notice placements.
WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility audit certificate for digital channels.
Employee signed acknowledgments confirming receipt of whistleblowing channel information.
Quarterly audit reports confirming channel functionality and accessibility.

key_requirements.text: Applies to companies with 50+ employees; public entities regardless of size
key_requirements.consequence: Insufficient staff training undermines compliance and may be cited in regulatory findings. Penalties: Fines up to EUR 1 million for retaliation against whistleblowers; fines up to EUR 300,000 for failure to establish reporting channels.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Training completion records; awareness programme materials; staff acknowledgement logs; competency assessments
how_to_meet: 1. Conduct an employee headcount verification as of the law's effective date and document the organisation's applicability status (≥50 employees = mandatory compliance; <50 employees = exemption).
2. If applicable (≥50 or public entity), establish a documented compliance scope covering all entities within the corporate group, ensuring consistent whistleblowing procedures across locations.
3. Maintain annual headcount reviews with signed HR attestations to trigger compliance adjustments if the threshold is crossed or if the organisation becomes a public entity.
internal_actions: HR team: provide official headcount certification signed by HR Director stating total employees as of the law's effective date and current date, including all employment types (permanent, fixed-term, part-time converted to FTE).

Legal team: document the organisation's applicability determination (in-scope/exempt) with written justification; confirm whether any group entities are public sector bodies requiring mandatory compliance regardless of size.
Finance team: certify organisational structure and any changes in legal entity status that would affect public sector classification.
Compliance team: establish an annual calendar reminder to review employee count and update applicability status with documented sign-off.
vendor_actions: Payroll/HR system provider: supply certified employee register exports showing total headcount, employment classification, and FTE calculations as of compliance date and current quarter.
External audit firm (if applicable): provide independent headcount verification report confirming threshold compliance for organisations with complex or multi-location structures.
compliance_evidence: Signed headcount certification from HR Director dated as of the law's effective date and current reporting period.
Written applicability determination letter (in-scope/exempt) signed by General Counsel.
Payroll system reports or certified HR register extracts showing employee count breakdown by location, employment type, and FTE.
Annual compliance scope reviews signed by HR and Legal with confirmation of threshold status or public entity classification.
Documentation of any group-wide applicability decisions affecting subsidiary or related entities.

regulations.penalties: Fines up to EUR 1 million for retaliation against whistleblowers; fines up to EUR 300,000 for failure to establish reporting channels

regulations.name: China Cybersecurity Law of the People's Republic of China
regulations.authority: Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC)
regulations.summary: China's Cybersecurity Law (CSL) is the foundational national cybersecurity legislation establishing requirements for network operators, critical information infrastructure (CII) operators, and data handlers. It mandates data localisation for CII operators, network security protections, user data handling restrictions, and real-name registration.

key_requirements.text: Collect and use personal information only with user consent, for lawful purposes, and on a necessity principle
key_requirements.consequence: Fines of CNY 50,000–500,000; suspension of business; licence revocation
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data collection policy; user agreement; privacy notice; minimisation assessment
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Conduct a data inventory audit to map all personal information collection points and document the lawful basis and necessity for each data element. 2. Implement a consent management system that captures explicit user opt-in before any personal data processing, with clear documentation of purposes. 3. Establish a Data Governance Committee to review and approve data usage policies annually, ensuring alignment with necessity principles.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Legal team: provide written data processing impact assessments (DPIAs) for each collection point and sign-off on lawful basis documentation. IT team: configure consent management platform settings and provide system access logs. Compliance team: maintain a register of all approved data uses and consent records.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Third-party data processors: sign Data Processing Agreements (DPAs) explicitly stating permitted uses, data retention limits, and sub-processor approval requirements. Consent platform vendor: provide audit reports confirming consent capture timestamps and non-repudiation controls.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Executed DPIAs, signed DPAs with all data processors, consent management system audit logs showing timestamp and user ID for each consent event, Data Governance Committee meeting minutes and approval records, data inventory spreadsheet with lawful basis mappings.

key_requirements.text: Classify network infrastructure and implement the Multi-Level Protection Scheme (MLPS 2.0) for your network security tier
key_requirements.consequence: Fines; suspension; licence revocation; criminal liability for executives
key_requirements.evidence_guide: MLPS classification certificate; protection documentation; security assessment reports
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Classify all network systems and data assets according to MLPS 2.0 taxonomy (Level 1–5) based on criticality and data sensitivity, documenting the classification rationale. 2. Design and implement security controls matching your assigned tier, including access controls, encryption, logging, and incident response capabilities. 3. Conduct a third-party MLPS 2.0 compliance assessment and obtain a formal evaluation report confirming your tier classification and control gaps.
key_requirements.internal_actions: IT team: provide network architecture diagrams, asset inventory, and system classification matrix aligned to MLPS 2.0. Security team: document all implemented technical controls (firewall rules, encryption standards, logging configurations, authentication mechanisms) in a control register. Compliance team: maintain the MLPS 2.0 evaluation report and remediation plan.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Qualified security assessment firm: conduct MLPS 2.0 compliance evaluation and issue a formal assessment report. Infrastructure vendors: provide security compliance certifications and configuration documentation for network equipment.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: MLPS 2.0 system classification document signed by management, third-party compliance assessment report with tier assignment, network architecture diagrams annotated with tier levels, technical control register with implementation dates, remediation plan for identified gaps.

key_requirements.text: Critical Information Infrastructure operators must store personal information and important data within mainland China
key_requirements.consequence: Fines; business suspension; criminal liability for senior management
key_requirements.evidence_guide: CII designation documents; data localisation architecture evidence; CAC assessment certificate
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Identify all systems and databases classified as Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) and determine which store personal data or important data. 2. Map the physical location of all data storage and establish data residency controls to ensure no replication or backup occurs outside mainland China. 3. Review and renegotiate contracts with all cloud and hosting vendors to explicitly prohibit offshore data transfer and mandate mainland China data center placement.
key_requirements.internal_actions: IT team: provide data residency audit report identifying all storage locations, backup destinations, and disaster recovery sites; implement technical controls (geo-blocking, storage location policies) to prevent offshore data movement. Legal team: issue directive to block any cloud service agreements that permit offshore data processing. Compliance team: maintain signed attestations from all system owners confirming mainland China data residency.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Cloud service providers and hosting vendors: sign amended Service Level Agreements (SLAs) with explicit mainland China data residency requirements, geographic data processing restrictions, and audit rights for verification. Vendors must provide written confirmation of data center locations and backup infrastructure.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Data residency audit report with storage location mappings, amended SLAs with mainland China residency clauses signed by all service providers, CII system inventory identifying personal and important data holders, vendor attestation letters confirming data center locations and backup policies, network configuration logs showing geo-blocking rules.

key_requirements.text: Report network security incidents to the competent authority within prescribed timelines
key_requirements.consequence: Fines; reputational damage; criminal liability
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Incident log; MIIT/CNCERT notification records; incident response procedures
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Define and document security incident classification criteria and mandatory reporting timelines (typically 8 hours for serious incidents to relevant authorities). 2. Establish an Incident Response Team with clear escalation procedures and draft incident notification templates for submission to cybersecurity authorities. 3. Conduct annual incident response drills to test reporting procedures and ensure all stakeholders understand timelines and submission channels.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Security team: develop and maintain an Incident Response Plan with defined severity levels, reporting timelines, and notification templates; provide incident log records and escalation decision documentation. Legal team: review incident notification content for regulatory compliance and accuracy before submission. Compliance team: maintain a register of all reported incidents with submission timestamps and authority acknowledgment receipts.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Managed Security Service Provider (if applicable): confirm incident detection, notification, and reporting SLAs align with regulatory timelines. Third-party forensics vendor: provide statement of availability for emergency incident investigation within required timeframes.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Signed Incident Response Plan with defined timelines and reporting procedures, incident classification policy approved by management, incident log showing date/time of detection and date/time of authority notification, email confirmations or receipts from cybersecurity authorities acknowledging incident reports, annual incident response drill records and test scenarios, notification templates with legal review sign-off.

regulations.penalties: Fines from RMB 10,000 to 1 million; suspension of operations; revocation of licences; criminal liability for serious violations

regulations.name: China Data Security Law 2021
regulations.authority: Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC) and sector regulators
regulations.summary: China's Data Security Law establishes a national data classification and grading system and imposes obligations on all data processing activities within China and overseas activities affecting national security. Introduces an Important Data catalogue system and mandatory security assessments before exporting important data.

key_requirements.text: Implement a data classification and grading system based on national importance and sensitivity
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Fines from RMB 100,000 to 10 million; suspension of operations; licence revocation for national security violations.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Establish a data classification framework defining four tiers (public, internal, sensitive, critical/national importance) aligned with CAC and industry guidelines. 2. Assign ownership to Data Governance team to classify all data assets and document classification rationale in a central registry. 3. Implement automated tagging in databases and file systems to enforce classification labels at point of collection and processing.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Legal team: provide written data classification policy with tier definitions and examples; IT team: deliver data asset inventory with assigned classifications and update frequency schedule; Compliance team: establish quarterly review schedule for classification accuracy.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: All vendors: confirm understanding of classification requirements and commit to honoring client-assigned data classifications in signed Data Processing Addendum; Cloud/storage providers: provide automated tagging/labeling capability and audit logs proving classification labels applied.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Signed data classification policy document; data asset inventory spreadsheet with classification tiers assigned; automated tag/label screenshots from systems; internal audit report validating classification completeness; vendor signed acknowledgment letters.

key_requirements.text: Identify and maintain an inventory of Important Data processed by the organisation
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Fines from RMB 100,000 to 10 million; suspension of operations; licence revocation for national security violations.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Define Important Data according to CAC guidance (data affecting state security, economic interests, public welfare, citizen rights). 2. Conduct full data mapping across all systems and business processes to identify all instances of Important Data, with ownership assigned per data custodian. 3. Maintain the inventory in a controlled register with retention schedules, processing purposes, and access controls documented.
key_requirements.internal_actions: IT team: provide system inventory and data flow diagrams; Business teams: submit data processing schedules and use-case documentation; Legal team: confirm Important Data definition aligned with CAC guidance and provide written determination for edge cases.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: All data processors: provide list of Important Data categories processed on client's behalf; cloud providers: confirm data location (mainland China requirement) and provide technical specifications for data residency controls.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Important Data inventory register (spreadsheet or database) with columns for data type, location, custodian, retention period, and processing purpose; CAC alignment memorandum signed by Legal; system mapping diagrams; vendor inventory submissions with signed acknowledgment.

key_requirements.text: Conduct national security reviews before any activity involving transfer of Important Data abroad
key_requirements.consequence: Failure to assess risks may constitute a compliance breach attracting regulatory action. Penalties: Fines from RMB 100,000 to 10 million; suspension of operations; licence revocation for national security violations.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Risk register; DPIA/PIA records; internal/external audit reports; risk treatment plans; review schedules
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Establish a national security review procedure requiring pre-approval before any cross-border transfer of Important Data, triggered during project initiation or vendor onboarding. 2. Conduct formal risk assessment covering geopolitical sensitivity, recipient jurisdiction, technical safeguards, and contract enforceability; document findings in a security review report. 3. Obtain approval from Legal and designated senior management before transfer, with evidence retained for audit trail.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Legal team: draft national security review template and approval workflow; Compliance team: establish transfer request log with review outcome, approver names, and dates; IT team: maintain audit logs showing all cross-border data movements and blocking of unapproved transfers.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Offshore vendors/partners: submit written attestation that they will not re-transfer Important Data without prior written approval; contract template must include clause prohibiting unauthorized cross-border transfer and imposing penalties for breach.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: National security review template and completed review report for each cross-border transfer; approval sign-off emails from Legal and management; transfer request log with dates and outcomes; vendor attestation letters; IT audit logs showing blocked/allowed transfers; signed contracts with transfer restriction clauses.

key_requirements.text: Apply enhanced protections to data that affects national security, public interests, or citizens' rights
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Fines from RMB 100,000 to 10 million; suspension of operations; licence revocation for national security violations.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Define enhanced protection requirements (encryption at rest/transit, access controls, audit logging) for data affecting national security, public interest, or citizen rights. 2. Apply technical controls: mandate encryption algorithms approved by state standards (SM2/SM4), implement multi-factor authentication for sensitive data access, enable immutable audit logs. 3. Conduct annual penetration testing and security assessments on systems holding sensitive data, with remediation of findings documented.
key_requirements.internal_actions: IT team: deploy encryption for data at rest and in transit, configure MFA for privileged access, enable detailed audit logging; Security team: conduct annual penetration test and security assessment, generate remediation plan with timelines; Infrastructure team: provide encryption key management procedures and backup/recovery documentation.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Cloud/infrastructure providers: confirm support for state-approved encryption algorithms (SM2/SM4) and provide encryption implementation specifications; vendors: commit to MFA support and provide audit log export capabilities in SLA.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Encryption deployment documentation with algorithm specifications; MFA configuration records; audit log samples showing access events and timestamps; annual penetration test report with findings and remediation status; vendor SLA addendum confirming security capabilities; encryption key management procedures document; state algorithm compliance certificate.

key_requirements.text: Report data security incidents affecting national security to relevant authorities
key_requirements.consequence: Failure to notify may result in regulatory fines and enforcement action. Penalties: Fines from RMB 100,000 to 10 million; suspension of operations; licence revocation for national security violations.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Incident response plan; breach notification logs; supervisor authority submission records; post-incident review reports
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Establish incident response procedure defining data security incidents (breach, unauthorized access, loss) affecting national security as mandatory-report events. 2. Create incident notification workflow specifying reporting timelines (72 hours initial notification, full details within 30 days), required information (incident type, data affected, impact assessment), and designated recipients (CAC, relevant authorities, impacted parties). 3. Maintain incident log with all disclosures, authority responses, and remediation actions taken.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Security team: develop incident response plan with national security incident escalation procedures; Legal team: identify relevant authorities by incident type and provide reporting contact list; Compliance team: establish incident tracking log with dates, content of reports, and authority acknowledgments.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: All vendors: commit to notifying client within 24 hours of detecting security incidents involving client data; vendors must provide incident details sufficient for client to assess national security impact and make regulatory disclosures.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Signed incident response plan with national security incident procedures; incident notification log showing incident date, report date, authority contacted, and reference numbers; copies of actual incident reports submitted to authorities (with sensitive details redacted as needed); vendor incident notification acknowledgment letters; authority receipt confirmations or response letters.

key_requirements.text: Build and maintain a data security management system with appropriate technical controls
key_requirements.consequence: Absence of required technical safeguards may result in enforcement notices and fines. Penalties: Fines from RMB 100,000 to 10 million; suspension of operations; licence revocation for national security violations.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Security configuration baselines; encryption certificates; vulnerability scan reports; penetration test results; patch management logs
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Design a data security management system (DSMS) framework with governance structure, policies, roles, and responsibilities; document in an internal standard or ISMS aligned with ISO 27001 adapted for PRC requirements. 2. Implement technical controls: network segmentation, access control lists, encryption, intrusion detection, and vulnerability management; baseline with CIS Controls or equivalent. 3. Conduct annual DSMS audit and management review to assess effectiveness, update policies, and demonstrate board-level oversight of data security.
key_requirements.internal_actions: IT/Security team: design and document DSMS framework including governance model, asset inventory, risk assessment methodology, and control baselines; deploy technical controls (segmentation, ACLs, encryption, IDS, patch management); Compliance team: schedule and conduct annual internal audit, prepare management review report; Board/Executive: sign off on annual data security management review and approve any policy updates.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Third-party auditors: perform annual DSMS audit and issue audit report with findings and recommendations; vendors handling sensitive data: provide proof of SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001 certification, or equivalent third-party security assessment.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: DSMS policy document and governance structure chart; technical control inventory with deployment evidence (network diagrams, access control matrices, encryption records); annual internal audit report; annual management review report signed by executives; third-party audit certificates (ISO 27001, SOC 2); vendor security assessment reports; remediation evidence for audit findings.

key_requirements.text: Cooperate with law enforcement investigations involving data security
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Fines from RMB 100,000 to 10 million; suspension of operations; licence revocation for national security violations.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
how_to_meet: 1. Establish a formal law enforcement liaison process with designated legal and IT contacts, including a 24/7 escalation protocol for urgent requests.
2. Document all law enforcement requests and responses in a centralized log, including request date, scope, data provided, and authorization basis.
3. Conduct annual training for legal, IT, and compliance teams on cooperation procedures, data protection during disclosure, and documentation requirements.
internal_actions: Legal team: draft and maintain law enforcement cooperation procedures and escalation matrix.
IT team: establish request intake system, data extraction protocols, and audit logs of all disclosures to authorities.
Compliance team: maintain a register of all law enforcement requests received, including dates, scope, and response status.
vendor_actions: Third-party service providers: provide contractual commitment to comply with Chinese law enforcement requests and to notify the organization immediately upon receipt of any direct request.
Cloud/hosting providers: confirm technical capability to provide data extracts within specified timeframes and maintain separate audit logs.
compliance_evidence: Signed law enforcement cooperation procedures and internal policy documentation.
Dated law enforcement request register with response timestamps and data disclosure records.
Training attendance logs and training materials for relevant personnel.
Vendor contracts with law enforcement cooperation clauses and signed acknowledgments.
Audit logs and system records demonstrating data extraction and disclosure capabilities.

key_requirements.text: Extraterritorial effect: applies to data processing abroad that harms Chinese national security
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Fines from RMB 100,000 to 10 million; suspension of operations; licence revocation for national security violations.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
how_to_meet: 1. Conduct a data mapping exercise to identify all processing activities that involve data of Chinese nationals or subjects relating to Chinese national security, regardless of server location.
2. Review all foreign subsidiaries, cloud providers, and offshore processing arrangements to assess compliance with Chinese DSL requirements even when technically outside China.

3. Implement contractual provisions in all international agreements requiring that processing comply with Chinese data security laws and obtain explicit vendor acknowledgment of extraterritorial scope.
internal_actions: Data governance team: create and maintain a global data inventory identifying data flows, processing locations, and data types (especially those involving Chinese nationals or sensitive national security contexts).
Legal team: review all subsidiary agreements, cloud contracts, and cross-border data transfer agreements to confirm inclusion of Chinese DSL compliance clauses.
IT/Security team: document technical controls applied to offshore systems to ensure equivalent protection standards to China-based processing.
vendor_actions: All overseas service providers and subsidiaries: sign amended contracts or undertakings explicitly acknowledging that China Data Security Law 2021 applies to their processing activities.
Cloud and hosting providers: confirm in writing that their terms of service and data security controls meet or exceed Chinese DSL requirements regardless of data residency location.
Third-party processors in jurisdictions outside China: provide documentation of their data security practices and certify compliance with Chinese legal obligations.
compliance_evidence: Global data inventory and mapping document showing all processing locations and data categories.
Amended or newly executed contracts with overseas subsidiaries, vendors, and cloud providers explicitly incorporating Chinese DSL compliance obligations.
Written confirmations from all foreign service providers acknowledging extraterritorial applicability of Chinese DSL.
Technical security assessment reports demonstrating equivalent controls applied to offshore systems.
Legal review memoranda confirming that all cross-border arrangements include required DSL compliance provisions.

regulations.penalties: Fines from RMB 100,000 to 10 million; suspension of operations; licence revocation for national security violations

regulations.name: Singapore Cybersecurity Act 2018
regulations.authority: Cyber Security Agency of Singapore (CSA)
regulations.summary: Singapore's Cybersecurity Act 2018, amended in 2024, establishes the regulatory framework for national cybersecurity including oversight of Critical Information Infrastructure (CII), licensure of cybersecurity service providers, and computer misuse provisions. The 2024 amendment expanded scope to include systems of temporary cybersecurity concern and major critical infrastructure.

key_requirements.text: Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) owners must notify CSA of cybersecurity incidents within 2 hours of awareness
key_requirements.consequence: Failure to notify may result in regulatory fines and enforcement action. Penalties: Fines up to SGD 100,000 and/or imprisonment up to 2 years for CII owners; fines up to SGD 500,000 for unlicensed cybersecurity services.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Incident response plan; breach notification logs; supervisor authority submission records; post-incident review reports
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Establish an incident detection and classification procedure that identifies cybersecurity incidents and triggers a 2-hour notification timer from first awareness. 2. Designate a CSA incident reporting contact and create a notification template with required incident details (nature, systems affected, impact assessment). 3. Document the notification process and maintain a log of all incidents reported with timestamps of awareness and CSA submission.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Legal team: prepare incident notification template and CSA contact registry. IT Security team: configure incident detection alerts and create incident log with awareness timestamps. Management: establish incident severity classification matrix.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: SOC provider (if applicable): provide contractual commitment to alert your organization of suspected incidents within 30 minutes of detection and confirm CSA notification responsibility assignment.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Signed incident notification procedures document, CSA incident report submissions with dated acknowledgment receipts, incident log showing detection timestamp and CSA notification timestamp (within 2 hours), and evidence of designated CSA contact appointment.

key_requirements.text: CII owners must conduct annual cybersecurity audits and risk assessments by a licensed professional
key_requirements.consequence: Failure to assess risks may constitute a compliance breach attracting regulatory action. Penalties: Fines up to SGD 100,000 and/or imprisonment up to 2 years for CII owners; fines up to SGD 500,000 for unlicensed cybersecurity services.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Risk register; DPIA/PIA records; internal/external audit reports; risk treatment plans; review schedules
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Identify and engage a CSA-licensed cybersecurity professional or audit firm to conduct an annual assessment covering risk identification, vulnerability testing, and control evaluation. 2. Schedule the audit for a fixed calendar period and document the scope covering all CII systems and data flows. 3. Retain the signed audit report and remediation plan, and track closure of all identified risks annually.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Procurement team: maintain current list of CSA-licensed audit providers and contract terms. IT Security team: provide systems inventory, network topology, and access control documentation to auditors. Management: approve audit scope and remediation budget.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Licensed audit firm: provide current CSA practitioner licence, signed audit engagement letter defining scope, and formal audit report with risk ratings and remediation recommendations.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Signed annual audit engagement letter, audit report dated within the compliance year, CSA practitioner licence copy of executing firm, remediation plan with owner assignments, and evidence of closure of prior-year findings.

key_requirements.text: CII owners must participate in CSA-directed cybersecurity exercises
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Fines up to SGD 100,000 and/or imprisonment up to 2 years for CII owners; fines up to SGD 500,000 for unlicensed cybersecurity services.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Establish a process to monitor CSA-issued cybersecurity exercise invitations and designate an internal exercise coordinator. 2. Confirm participation within CSA's required timeframe and document assigned team members, roles, and test objectives. 3. Conduct post-exercise reviews, document findings, and retain evidence of participation and performance metrics.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Security Operations team: assign exercise coordinator and incident response personnel. Management: confirm budget and resource allocation for exercise participation. Legal/Compliance: maintain calendar of CSA exercise schedules and invitations.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: External exercise facilitators (if CSA-contracted): provide exercise briefing materials, scenario details, observation schedule, and formal attendance/performance report.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: CSA exercise invitation and acceptance email, participant roster with assigned roles, exercise scenario documentation, post-exercise report with findings, and attendance sign-in records or digital participation logs.

key_requirements.text: Obtain licences from CSA for providing specified cybersecurity services (penetration testing, SOC, forensics)
key_requirements.consequence: Absence of required technical safeguards may result in enforcement notices and fines. Penalties: Fines up to SGD 100,000 and/or imprisonment up to 2 years for CII owners; fines up to SGD 500,000 for unlicensed cybersecurity services.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Security configuration baselines; encryption certificates; vulnerability scan reports; penetration test results; patch management logs
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Confirm whether your organization provides penetration testing, Security Operations Centre services, or digital forensics to clients; if yes, identify the CSA licensing requirement tier. 2. Prepare an application package including practitioner credentials, technical capability documentation, and quality assurance procedures. 3. Submit the application to CSA and maintain the issued licence with annual renewal and compliance audits.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Service Delivery team: document technical service offerings and scope. HR/Compliance: compile employee cybersecurity certifications (OSCP, GCIH, GPEN, CEH). Quality Assurance team: prepare service standard operating procedures and audit logs.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Subcontracted service providers: submit CSA licence copies and maintain licensed status; provide audit trail of service delivery and compliance with CSA-mandated standards.
