key_requirements.evidence_guide: Policy register; Records of Processing Activities; consent management records; privacy notices; data subject request logs
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Establish a centralized repository (digital or physical) for all safety management records including hazard assessments, training logs, inspection reports, and incident investigations, with defined retention schedules per SAPA statute of limitations. 2. Implement an accident response protocol with documented procedures for notification, investigation, remediation, and communication, tested through annual tabletop drills. 3. Assign records custodian responsibility and implement access controls to ensure authenticity and prevent unauthorized alteration.
key_requirements.internal_actions: HR team: maintain employee safety training records with dates, attendees, and trainer signatures covering all hazard-specific and emergency response training. IT team: configure document management system with audit trails, access logs, and immutable timestamping for all safety records; provide quarterly export reports of record retention and access activity. Legal team: document SAPA-compliant record retention policy specifying minimum retention periods and archival procedures; provide template for incident investigation reports.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Document management service providers: confirm system capability for immutable records storage, tamper-evident logging, and SAPA-compliant retention periods; provide service level agreement with audit trail export functionality. Occupational health consultants: deliver completed safety audits, inspection checklists, and incident investigation templates aligned to SAPA requirements.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Records retention policy signed by management; sample safety training rosters with attendance signatures and dates; incident investigation reports with root cause analysis and corrective actions; document management system audit trail exports showing record creation dates and access history; quarterly records completeness reports; inspection checklists and hazard assessment documents stamped with dates; emergency response protocol documentation with drill attendance records and after-action reports.

regulations.penalties: CEOs and responsible managers: up to 1 year imprisonment (mandatory) and fines up to KRW 1 billion for fatal accidents; company fines up to KRW 5 billion

regulations.name: UK Environment Act 2021
regulations.authority: Office for Environmental Protection (OEP) and Environment Agency
regulations.summary: The UK Environment Act 2021 sets legally binding targets for air quality, water quality, biodiversity, and waste reduction following Brexit. It establishes the Office for Environmental Protection (OEP) as the enforcement body. Key supply chain requirements include due diligence obligations to prevent UK businesses from using commodities produced on illegally deforested land.

key_requirements.text: Businesses using specified commodities (cattle, cocoa, coffee, palm oil, soya, rubber) must conduct due diligence to prevent illegal deforestation in supply chains
key_requirements.consequence: Unlawful data transfers or inadequate vendor contracts expose the organisation to fines. Penalties: Civil sanctions from the OEP; fines and injunctions; unlimited fines for deforestation due diligence failures.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Vendor contracts/DPAs; third-party risk assessments; supplier due diligence records; data transfer agreements
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Map all direct and indirect suppliers of cattle, cocoa, coffee, palm oil, soya, and rubber; document sourcing geography and production dates. 2. Conduct risk assessment of each supplier against known deforestation hotspots and cross-reference with satellite imagery or third-party deforestation databases. 3. Establish contractual deforestation clauses requiring suppliers to certify no illegal clearing occurred after the commodity cutoff date (typically 31 December 2020).
key_requirements.internal_actions: Procurement team: produce commodity supply chain register with supplier names, locations, and commodity volumes. Legal team: draft standard supplier deforestation warranty clause for inclusion in purchase agreements. Compliance team: document risk assessment methodology and scoring matrix used to classify suppliers by deforestation risk.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Suppliers: provide signed declarations of legal land use and non-deforestation compliance, forest certification documents (e.g. FSC, RSPO), and geo-tagged production location evidence. Third-party auditors: deliver independent audit reports verifying supplier deforestation claims where high-risk indicators are present.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Signed supplier deforestation warranties, commodity traceability register with dates and locations, risk assessment matrix with supplier classifications, third-party audit reports, satellite imagery analysis reports or deforestation database query results, and supply chain mapping diagrams.

key_requirements.text: Maintain records of commodity supply chains sufficient to demonstrate absence of illegal deforestation
key_requirements.consequence: Unlawful data transfers or inadequate vendor contracts expose the organisation to fines. Penalties: Civil sanctions from the OEP; fines and injunctions; unlimited fines for deforestation due diligence failures.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Vendor contracts/DPAs; third-party risk assessments; supplier due diligence records; data transfer agreements
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Implement a centralized traceability system (digital or documented) capturing supplier identity, commodity type, volume, origin country, and production date for each shipment. 2. Establish document retention policy requiring records be held for minimum 7 years and stored in secure, backed-up location. 3. Conduct quarterly audits of records completeness and accuracy against transaction logs.
key_requirements.internal_actions: IT team: deploy or configure supply chain visibility platform with audit trail functionality and role-based access controls. Records management: define data retention schedule and archiving procedures. Finance team: ensure invoices and purchase orders are linked to commodity traceability records.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Suppliers: provide invoices, bills of lading, and certificates of origin documenting commodity details for each shipment. Logistics partners: furnish shipping manifests and port of origin documentation.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Centralized supply chain register with complete transaction records, system access logs and audit trails, document retention policy, quarterly completeness audit reports, sample invoices/bills of lading, and certificates of origin for auditor inspection.

key_requirements.text: Report annually on due diligence steps taken for forest risk commodities
key_requirements.consequence: Unlawful data transfers or inadequate vendor contracts expose the organisation to fines. Penalties: Civil sanctions from the OEP; fines and injunctions; unlimited fines for deforestation due diligence failures.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Vendor contracts/DPAs; third-party risk assessments; supplier due diligence records; data transfer agreements
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Compile annual due diligence report documenting all deforestation prevention actions, supplier assessments conducted, and remediation of non-compliant suppliers. 2. Include statement of commodity volumes sourced, geographic origin breakdown, and percentage of supply chain verified through audit or certification. 3. Obtain sign-off from senior management and publish or file report as required by regulatory deadline (typically end of financial year plus 4 months).
key_requirements.internal_actions: Compliance team: consolidate due diligence evidence and prepare annual report template. Legal team: review report for regulatory accuracy and legal sufficiency. Finance team: verify commodity volume and sourcing data from procurement records. Board/Leadership: approve and sign off on report before submission.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Suppliers and auditors: provide summary confirmation of compliance status and any remediation actions taken during the reporting period.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Signed annual due diligence report filed with regulator (or retained if not publicly filed), report containing due diligence methodology, supplier assessment summaries, remediation actions log, commodity volume attestations, and board approval memo.

key_requirements.text: Comply with legally binding targets for fine particulate matter, water quality, and biodiversity
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Civil sanctions from the OEP; fines and injunctions; unlimited fines for deforestation due diligence failures.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Identify applicable environmental targets relevant to your operations (fine particulate matter emissions, water discharge quality, biodiversity impact) and establish baseline measurements using accredited testing. 2. Implement monitoring and measurement procedures with defined frequency; maintain calibrated equipment and qualified personnel. 3. Report actual performance against targets annually; where non-compliant, document root cause and corrective action plan with timelines.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Environmental/Operations team: establish baseline measurements and monitoring schedule; document procedures and responsible parties. Quality/Compliance team: maintain equipment calibration records and personnel competency certifications. Finance team: budget for ongoing testing and reporting.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Testing laboratories: provide accredited environmental testing reports (PM2.5 analysis, water quality sampling results, biodiversity surveys) with traceability and quality assurance documentation.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Baseline measurement reports, monitoring and measurement procedures manual, calibration certificates for monitoring equipment, personnel competency records, environmental test reports from accredited labs, annual compliance reports showing actual vs. target performance, and documented corrective action plans for any breaches.

key_requirements.text: Local authorities must meet new environmental reporting requirements
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Civil sanctions from the OEP; fines and injunctions; unlimited fines for deforestation due diligence failures.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Review published environmental reporting guidance from Defra/relevant regulator and identify mandatory metrics (air quality, water quality, biodiversity, waste). 2. Establish data collection processes across all local authority functions and integrate with existing systems; assign reporting ownership. 3. Prepare and submit annual environmental report within published deadline, ensuring data accuracy through internal review and sign-off.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Environmental/Sustainability team: define reporting scope, data sources, and collection procedures for each environmental metric. IT team: integrate data collection into existing management systems or create manual reporting templates. Finance/Admin: allocate resources and budget for reporting process. Leadership: approve report before submission.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: External data providers (e.g. air/water quality agencies): confirm data access agreements and data sharing protocols to ensure timely provision of required datasets.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Published annual environmental report filed with regulator, data collection procedures and templates, completed data collection logs, internal review and sign-off memos, and evidence of timely submission.

key_requirements.text: Producer responsibility obligations for packaging and waste management
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Civil sanctions from the OEP; fines and injunctions; unlimited fines for deforestation due diligence failures.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Audit packaging portfolio and waste streams; classify own-brand packaging and quantify annual tonnage placed on market. 2. Calculate producer responsibility financial contribution or recycling tonnage obligation based on packaging tonnage and register with approved compliance scheme. 3. Maintain records of packaging material type, weight, and destination (reuse, recycling, disposal) and submit evidence to compliance scheme annually.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Procurement/Operations team: audit packaging specifications, suppliers, and quantities; create packaging register by material type (plastic, cardboard, glass, metal). Compliance team: register with compliant scheme and calculate annual obligation. Finance team: process required contributions or identify recycled tonnage commitments.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: Packaging suppliers: provide weight/material composition documentation for each packaging component. Approved compliance scheme: provide registration, obligation calculation, and annual reporting templates. Waste/Recycling contractors: furnish evidence of recycled tonnage achieved and destination of packaging waste.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Packaging audit report with material type and tonnage breakdown, compliance scheme registration certificate, annual obligation calculation, packaging register maintained throughout year, invoices/statements from compliance scheme, waste contractor reports showing recycled tonnage, and scheme annual declaration filing.

key_requirements.text: OEP can investigate public authorities for environmental law compliance failures
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Civil sanctions from the OEP; fines and injunctions; unlimited fines for deforestation due diligence failures.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Establish an internal environmental compliance monitoring function to track adherence to relevant environmental legislation and OEP enforcement notices. 2. Create a documented procedure for responding to OEP investigation requests, including timelines for evidence submission and escalation protocols. 3. Maintain a register of all environmental law obligations applicable to your organisation and conduct quarterly compliance gap assessments.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Legal team: provide current register of environmental law obligations and OEP enforcement notices received; Compliance team: document investigation response procedures and SLAs; Internal Audit: schedule quarterly environmental compliance reviews and retain assessment reports.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: External counsel (if applicable): provide written confirmation of environmental law exposure assessment; Environmental consultants: deliver compliance audit reports identifying any current or potential failures.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Environmental compliance register, OEP correspondence log, investigation response procedures (signed and dated), quarterly compliance assessment reports, external counsel opinion letters, environmental audit reports with timestamps.

key_requirements.text: Applies to companies using forest risk commodities with an annual UK turnover above threshold
key_requirements.consequence: Non-compliance with data governance obligations may attract regulatory fines and orders. Penalties: Civil sanctions from the OEP; fines and injunctions; unlimited fines for deforestation due diligence failures.
key_requirements.evidence_guide: Data inventory/classification register; processing records; data lifecycle policy; records of lawful basis
key_requirements.how_to_meet: 1. Conduct a turnover threshold assessment for the last financial year to determine if mandatory forest risk commodity due diligence applies to your organisation. 2. If threshold is exceeded, map all commodities in your supply chain (soy, beef, palm, wood, cocoa, rubber) and identify high-risk supplier jurisdictions. 3. Implement documented due diligence procedures covering traceability, risk assessment, and remediation actions for non-compliant suppliers.
key_requirements.internal_actions: Finance team: provide certified turnover figures and threshold calculation workings; Supply Chain team: deliver commodity mapping spreadsheet and supplier jurisdiction risk matrix; Procurement team: document due diligence procedures and supplier questionnaire templates; Legal team: confirm applicability determination in writing.
key_requirements.vendor_actions: All forest risk commodity suppliers: provide supply chain traceability documentation, deforestation risk certifications, and signed declarations of non-involvement in illegal land conversion; Third-party assurance providers: deliver audit reports confirming supplier compliance with due diligence standards.
key_requirements.compliance_evidence: Turnover threshold calculation (audited), applicability determination letter, commodity mapping register with jurisdiction risk ratings, supplier due diligence questionnaires and signed responses, traceability documentation, third-party audit reports, remediation records for non-compliant suppliers.

regulations.penalties: Civil sanctions from the OEP; fines and injunctions; unlimited fines for deforestation due diligence failures
